title: wrd.jar
text: wrd.jar The Java archive file wrd.jar contains the Uniface Java servlets—the Web Request Dispatcher (WRD) for deploying web applications and SOAP Request Dispatcher (SRD) for SOAP-based web services. Description The Uniface servlets handle HTTP and SOAP requests received by the web server that need to be sent to Uniface. Checking the Version Number The wrd.jar file is updated with each Uniface release, and sometimes in patches and service packs. To check the version that you are using, you can do one of the following: In a web browser, enter the URL for the WRD_Version (for example: http:localhost:8080/uniface/wrd/WRD_Version ). If necessary, replace localhost with the host ID and 8080 with the port number of the web server in which the servlet is running. Extract and open the manifest.mf file in the wrd.jar . By default, wrd.jar is located in the /common/tomcat/lib directory of your Uniface installation. Updating the latest wrd.jar with patch 10.4.03 manually For avoiding compatibility issues, the latest WRD module wrd.jar will not be copied automatically in patching process. Users need to manually update it by performing the following steps: Download the patch release 10.4.03.000 (or higher) and install it. If your Uniface installation folder name is, for example - UNIFACE_HOME , and tomcat install folder is TOMCAT_HOME (by default, it should be UNIFACE_HOME\common\tomcat ): If you have Tomcat 9 installed in TOMCAT_HOME , copy UNIFACE_HOME\uniface\webapps\uniface\WEB-INF\lib_wrd\v6\wrd.jar into TOMCAT_HOME\lib\ . If you have Tomcat 10.1 installed in TOMCAT_HOME , copy UNIFACE_HOME\uniface\webapps\uniface\WEB-INF\lib_wrd\v7\wrd.jar into TOMCAT_HOME\lib\ . Related Links SOAP Request Dispatcher (SRD) The Uniface SOAP Request Dispatcher (SRD) is a Java servlet that is responsible for brokering SOAP and WSDL requests for web services. Web Request Dispatcher (WRD) The Uniface Web Request Dispatcher (WRD) is used to deploy Uniface web applications. It is a Java servlet that is responsible for brokering requests and maintaining connections between web servers and the Uniface Router. The WRD communicates with the Uniface Server via the Uniface Router. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
